title: Cisco Identity Intelligence (CII) Risk-Score Drift and Adaptive-Auth Triggering

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(j) covers MFA / continuous-auth solutions. CII-driven adaptive auth is one such solution; integration health evidences operational soundness.
IA-10 (Adaptive Authentication) — CII-driven adaptive auth is a direct implementation; integration health evidences operational continuity.
A.8.5 (Secure authentication) — adaptive auth is an authentication approach; CII integration health evidences operational soundness.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Misconfigure CII API credentials. ISE emits 82502 on next sync attempt. UC-17.1.46 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restore credentials. Sync succeeds. Alert clears.

dataSources: Cisco Identity Intelligence (CII) is a cloud service introduced 2024 that derives identity risk scores from cross-source telemetry (Duo, ISE, Cisco Secure Endpoint, MS Entra). Integration with ISE is via pxGrid Cloud (UC-17.1.35). Once integrated, ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Identity Intelligence* category — codes `82500` (CII risk score updated for endpoint), `82501` (CII high-risk score triggered adaptive auth), `82502` (CII feed import failed), `82503` (CII adapter cert error). Fields: `endpoint_user`, `risk_score`, `risk_change`, `triggered_action`. Direct CII REST API at `/api/v1/identity/risk` polled by a Splunk modular input.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (82500, 82501, 82502, 82503))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:cii")
| stats count, latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode=="82502")) as feed_failures, count(eval(MessageCode=="82503")) as cert_errors, count(eval(MessageCode=="82501")) as triggered_actions by endpoint_user
| where feed_failures > 0 OR cert_errors > 0 OR triggered_actions > 0
| sort - triggered_actions
description: Tracks Cisco Identity Intelligence integration health (feed import, cert validity) and audits cases where CII risk-score elevation triggered adaptive auth (step-up MFA, additional verification, quarantine).
value: CII is the consolidation point for cross-platform identity risk; when the integration breaks, ISE adaptive-auth decisions revert to ISE-only signals. Auditing the CII-triggered actions provides evidence the cross-source enrichment is materially driving access decisions.
implementation: Confirm *Identity Intelligence* category at INFO. Deploy CII REST API poller every 30 min. Schedule the search every 15 min, search window `-1h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0.
visualization: (1) Single value: triggered actions last 24h. (2) Trend: avg risk-score per identity over 30d. (3) Top-N: identities with most triggered actions.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **CII service maintenance** — Cisco-side CII maintenance briefly drops feed; cross-reference Cisco trust-center status. (b) **Initial onboarding** — First days of CII deployment, score baselines are stabilising; many adaptive-auth triggers may fire as the model calibrates.
mitreAttack: N/A (identity-store hygiene — measures Cisco Identity Intelligence feed health, no attack mapping)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Identity Intelligence subscription active.
• ISE 3.3+ with CII integration configured.
• `cii_high_risk_actions.csv` lookup of accepted adaptive-auth actions.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Identity Intelligence* category at INFO. Deploy CII REST API poller.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 15 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — CII Adaptive Auth":
• Single value: triggered actions.
• Trend: avg risk score.
• Top-N: triggered identities.

Alerting: medium PagerDuty on cert_error or sustained feed_failures.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Feed failures every 30 min** — API credential rotation or token expiry.
• **No 82500 events** — Category at WARN; lower to INFO.
• **Triggered_actions = 0 over 30d** — CII feed working but ISE adaptive-auth policy not consuming the score; review policy conditions.
grandmaExplanation: Cisco's identity-risk service combines signals from multiple security tools and gives each user a risk score. We watch the link to that service — and ring the bell if the link breaks or if it triggers extra security checks for users we should know about.
